summary: Persistent privileged remote-command agent with continuous host/session exfiltration
details: claw-subagent-service installs a privileged background service (Windows SCM via node-windows postinstall when run as admin; systemd unit at /etc/systemd/system/claw-subagent-service.service on Linux; launchd plist at /Library/LaunchDaemons/claw-subagent-service.plist on macOS) that connects out to a publisher-controlled RongCloud IM channel (token endpoint https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, appKey 'bmdehs6pbyyks', api.rong-api.com) and dispatches inbound IM messages as host commands. service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js routes RongyunMessageTypeEnum.COMMAND / device_control / chat_message into handleCommand → executeCommand via ScriptExecutor, granting the operator on the other end of the IM channel arbitrary command execution as root/SYSTEM (start/stop/restart/uninstall service, delete local config, spawn shell scripts, forward chat to a local 'openclaw' agent CLI). In parallel, service/modules/dashboard-collector.js continuously harvests host MAC address, OS info, and the contents of ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl (assistant message records including model, provider, tokens, cost, sessionId, agentId), ~/.openclaw/projects/projects.json, and ~/.openclaw/tasks/tasks.json, and ships them back through the same IM channel on a heartbeat (not user-initiated). service/updater.js additionally polls the npm registry every 6 hours and on Windows runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` unattended, then restarts the worker — anyone who later controls the npm name can silently push new code into existing privileged installs. The package.json description ('虾说智能助手' / 'intelligent assistant') does not disclose any of this remote-control or telemetry behavior to a developer running `sudo npm i -g`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
